risks.risk: Rural Patagonia internet connectivity severely limited — over 40% of rural communities have no internet access, and 67.5% of Argentine households are reducing telecom expenses due to inflation
risks.mitigation: Design offline-first mobile app with sync-when-connected architecture; explore Starlink partnerships for key producer clusters
risks.evidence: TS2 Tech 2025 report: 40%+ rural communities with no internet; 67.5% households reducing telecom expenses

risks.risk: Ultra-narrow customer segment — only ~500-2,000 artisan goat cheese producers in all of Patagonia, 85% informal family operations with minimal tech infrastructure
risks.mitigation: Expand target to all artisan dairy producers in Argentina (not just goat cheese, not just Patagonia) to increase addressable base
risks.evidence: Market dept SOM: $15K-$120K for Patagonia-only segment; Problem dept: 85% informal operations

risks.risk: No regulatory tailwind for goat traceability — SENASA Resolution 530/2025 makes livestock traceability mandatory for bovines/equines from 2026 but voluntary for goats, sheep, camelids, and pigs
risks.mitigation: Monitor SENASA regulatory roadmap; position product as voluntary best-practice tool rather than compliance necessity
risks.evidence: SENASA Resolution 530/2025 via Tridge and Bichos de Campo

risks.risk: Target customers have extremely low willingness/ability to pay — informal family operations with no evidence of spending on software; competitive pricing gap at ultra-low end (<$25/mo) confirms unserved segment but may be unserved because it's unprofitable
risks.mitigation: Explore cooperative/association-level licensing or government subsidy programs for digitalization; freemium model with premium compliance features
risks.evidence: Competitive dept: pricing gap at ultra-low end; no WTP statements found in Problem dept

risks.risk: MVP development cost ($30K+) disproportionate to market opportunity (SOM $15K-$120K/year) — payback period could exceed product lifetime
risks.mitigation: Use open-source components (InvenTree) to reduce dev costs; validate with 5-10 paid pilots before full build
risks.evidence: SaaS development cost guides: MVP ~$30K minimum; Market dept SOM estimate

risks.risk: Artisan cheese production in Patagonia is economically fragile — producers face inflation, limited market access, and seasonal production cycles that make recurring SaaS payments unreliable
risks.mitigation: Offer annual billing with harvest-season payment; explore government agtech subsidy integration
risks.evidence: Argentine hyperinflation context; 85% informal operations from Problem dept

risks.risk: Provincial regulatory fragmentation — artisanal dairy authorization handled by provincial and municipal authorities, not just SENASA, creating inconsistent requirements across Patagonia's provinces
risks.mitigation: Start with one province (e.g., Chubut or Neuquén) and build province-specific compliance modules
risks.evidence: Argentina.gob.ar artisanal dairy regulation: provincial/municipal authorities handle authorization

risks.risk: No established digital channels to reach target producers — no online communities with 1,000+ members, no industry conferences specific to Patagonian artisan goat cheese
risks.mitigation: Partner with INTA (agricultural extension) and provincial development agencies for distribution; attend regional fairs
risks.evidence: Market dept: 0 early adopter segments meeting all 3 criteria (label, spend evidence, reachable channel)

dependencies.fallback: Offline-first architecture with sync; Starlink satellite (authorized in Argentina Feb 2024, expanding via Telespazio/Altec partnership)
dependencies.history: Over 40% of rural communities had no internet as of 2020-2021 study; Starlink and government REFEFO backbone expanding but incomplete

dependencies.dependency: Artisan goat cheese producer segment in Patagonia
dependencies.fallback: Expand to all artisan dairy producers across Argentina; or expand to all small food producers
dependencies.history: Segment is ~500-2,000 producers, 85% informal, with only 4% of caprine stock formally declared via electronic transit documents

dependencies.dependency: SENASA regulatory status quo (goat traceability voluntary)
dependencies.fallback: If made mandatory, becomes tailwind; product works as voluntary best-practice tool regardless
dependencies.history: SENASA has been progressively tightening traceability: phytosanitary products mandatory April 2025, bovine/equine mandatory Jan 2026; goats may follow

dependencies.dependency: Argentine economic stability (peso inflation, purchasing power)
dependencies.fallback: USD-denominated pricing with local currency option; government subsidy programs for digitalization
dependencies.history: Argentina's hyperinflation cycles repeatedly impact SaaS adoption; 67.5% of households reducing telecom expenses in 2025

top_3_killers.risk: Rural Patagonia internet connectivity gap makes SaaS deployment nearly impossible for 40%+ of target users
top_3_killers.why_killer: A SaaS product requires internet. If the target users physically cannot access the internet, the product has no delivery mechanism. Offline-first is a workaround but fundamentally changes the product complexity and cost.
top_3_killers.early_warning_signal: Pilot users unable to complete onboarding or sync data within first week of trial

top_3_killers.risk: Ultra-narrow customer segment ($15K-$120K SOM) makes the business economically unviable as a standalone venture
top_3_killers.why_killer: Even at 100% market capture, revenue would not cover a single developer's salary. The market is too small to sustain a dedicated product without dramatic scope expansion.
top_3_killers.early_warning_signal: Fewer than 20 producers express interest in paid pilots within first 3 months of outreach

top_3_killers.risk: Target customers have near-zero willingness/ability to pay for software — informal family operations with no history of software spending
top_3_killers.why_killer: No evidence of willingness-to-pay was found in any complaint thread. If customers won't pay $25-50/mo, there is no business model. Free tools or government-provided solutions would be needed.
top_3_killers.early_warning_signal: Zero conversions from free tier to paid within 6 months of launch
